title: steireya cordata
intro: Steireya cordata, also known as the Siam tulip or Steirachys sanguinea, is a flowering plant native to Southeast Asia. Its medicinal properties lie in its rhizome, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Steireya cordata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and swelling, and even exhibit antiviral and antifungal activities. Herbal preparations of Steireya cordata often involve drying and powdering the rhizome, which can then be used in teas, tinctures, or capsules to harness its medicinal properties.
